summary: Ukrainian air defenses reported shooting down 150 hostile UAVs across multiple fronts, while Russia intensified strikes on logistics hubs including the UN food depot in Dnipro and the Rosneft refinery in Tuapse. Belarus is alleged to host Russian drone launch preparations targeting the Kiev‑Chop highway. Heavy civilian casualties continue from Russian attacks, with 70‑100 civilians killed weekly.
key_points: Ukrainian forces downed 150 enemy Shahed, Gerbera, Italmash and other UAVs on the night of May 27, with hits recorded in seven locations (Source: @kpszsu/62966).
Russia may launch drone strikes on the M‑06 Kiev‑Chop highway from Belarus, aiming at western supply routes (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30460).
A Russian Iskander missile destroyed the UN World Food Programme warehouse in Dnipro, eliminating food for 130,000 people (Source: @UkraineNow/81473).
The Rosneft refinery in Tuapse suffered its fifth drone strike of the spring, causing a fire at the marine terminal (Source: @eRadarrua/93884).
Belarusian officials claimed 116 Ukrainian drone incursions across their border in one week, a claim denied by Ukrainian officials (Source: @UkraineNow/81476).
Ukrainian Ministry of Internal Affairs reported 70‑100 civilian deaths per week from Russian drone, ballistic and cruise missile attacks (Source: @UkraineNow/81472).
Russian forces captured the villages of Vozdvizhevka (Zaporizhzhia region) and Granov (Kharkiv region) (Source: @tass_agency/377761).

sections.executive_summary: Ukrainian air defenses downed 150 hostile UAVs across the north, south and east, reflecting intense aerial combat. Russia escalated strikes on critical logistics, destroying the UN food depot in Dnipro and repeatedly hitting the Rosneft refinery in Tuapse. Belarus is reportedly being used as a launch base for drones targeting the Kiev‑Chop highway, widening the conflict’s geographic scope.

points: Ukrainian air defense units, supported by aviation, anti‑aircraft missile troops, electronic warfare units and mobile fire groups, shot down or suppressed 150 Shahed, Gerbera, Italmash and other UAVs, with eight UAVs hitting seven locations and debris falling in four locations across the country (Source: @kpszsu/62966).

The Ukrainian General Staff reported that Russian forces attacked Crimea, shooting down at least 14 UAVs in Sevastopol and launching air‑based missiles at a power station without causing outages (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30461).
Ukrainian officials announced ongoing negotiations for a "1000 on 1000" prisoner exchange, including long‑term detainees and severely wounded prisoners (Source: @UkraineNow/81490).

Ukrainian authorities warned civilians in Kherson region of deliberate drone attacks on cars and infrastructure, noting two civilian deaths and four injuries in recent strikes (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30461).

Ukrainian forces reported advances on multiple fronts, including pushing Russian units out of Ryasnoe village on the Sumy front and gaining up to 900 m in twenty‑two sectors (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30461).
Russian forces captured the villages of Vozdvizhevka in Zaporizhzhia region and Granov in Kharkiv region, according to the Ministry of Defense (Source: @tass_agency/377761).
Russia conducted a missile strike on the UN food depot in Dnipro with an Iskander system, destroying supplies for 130 000 people valued at $1.4 million (Source: @UkraineNow/81473).

The Rosneft refinery in Tuapse was hit for the fifth time this spring by a drone attack, causing a fire at the marine terminal and involving over 80 personnel and 25 pieces of equipment in firefighting (Source: @eRadarrua/93884).
Russian air defenses reported increased intensity of aircraft, artillery and strike UAV usage in the Konstantinovka area, indicating heightened offensive operations (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30461).

Russian forces launched UAV attacks on the Sevastopol area, shooting down 14 UAVs and attempting to strike a power station, though Ukrainian air defense prevented outages (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30461).
Ukrainian reports indicate Russian UAV attacks from Kursk, Bryansk, Oryol, Millerovo and Shatalovo directions, with 150 UAVs downed across the north, south and east (Source: @kpszsu/62966).

On the Sumy front, Ukrainian forces advanced up to 900 m in twenty‑two sectors, retaking villages such as Zapselye and engaging in firefights near Lesnoye and Taratutino (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30461).
In the Donetsk region, Ukrainian air defense operated against Russian attacks, though specific outcomes were not detailed (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30461).
Russian units of the Eastern Military District penetrated northwest of Alexandrovgrad in Dnipropetrovsk region, conducting offensive actions beyond the Volchya River (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30461).
Ukrainian forces reported UAV activity near Chernihiv (Snovsk) and Zhytomyr (Radvil) indicating continued aerial contest in the eastern sector (Source: @kpszsu/62961).
Ukrainian air defenses downed 150 hostile UAVs, including Shahed and Gerbera types, with engagements reported in the north (Source: @kpszsu/62966).
UAVs were observed circling near Chernihiv (Nizhyn area) and in the vicinity of Kyiv, including three UAVs over the Chernobyl exclusion zone (Source: @eRadarrua/93891, @eRadarrua/93917).
Ukrainian forces reported a UAV heading from Kyiv region toward Zhytomyr, targeting Ovrych (Source: @kpszsu/62978).
Russian attacks from Kursk and Bryansk directions targeted northern Ukrainian airspace, prompting multi‑layered defense responses (Source: @kpszsu/62966).
Ukrainian reports noted UAV activity over the Snovsk‑Beremna‑Kulikivka corridor in Chernihiv region (Source: @eRadarrua/93867).

Russian attacks on the southern transport corridor included multiple strikes on Energodar, Kamensk‑Dneprovsky, Berdyansk, Tokmak, Vasilevka and Mykhailivka districts, damaging civilian cars and infrastructure (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30461).
Ukrainian forces reported increased pressure from the southern flank, with fighting west of Grishino and in Novoalexandrovka and Vasilevka (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30461).
The Russian‑controlled city of Sevastopol experienced UAV attacks, with 14 UAVs shot down and missile strikes on a power station (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30461).
Russian missile strikes targeted the UN food depot in Dnipro, a key logistics hub for frontline support (Source: @UkraineNow/81473).
Ukrainian reports indicated Russian UAVs operating over Kherson region, deliberately targeting civilian vehicles (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30461).
Ukrainian air defense claimed 150 UAVs shot down on the night of May 27, with eight UAVs hitting seven locations across the country (Source: @kpszsu/62966).
Russian forces reportedly used Iskander missiles to strike the UN warehouse in Dnipro (Source: @UkraineNow/81473).
Ukrainian reports of Russian UAV attacks from multiple directions (Kursk, Bryansk, Oryol, Millerovo, Shatalovo) indicate a broad aerial offensive (Source: @kpszsu/62966).
In Crimea, Ukrainian forces downed at least 14 UAVs in Sevastopol and intercepted air‑based missiles aimed at a power station (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30461).
Russian air defenses increased activity in Konstantinovka, suggesting heightened aerial engagements in that sector (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30461).

key_developments: Ukrainian air defenses shot down 150 hostile UAVs, including Shahed, Gerbera and Italmash types, across the north, south and east on May 27 (Source: @kpszsu/62966).
Russia is preparing drone launches from Belarus to strike the M‑06 Kiev‑Chop highway, a key western supply route (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30460).
An Iskander missile destroyed the UN World Food Programme depot in Dnipro, eliminating food supplies for 130 000 people (Source: @UkraineNow/81473).
The Rosneft refinery in Tuapse suffered its fifth drone strike of the spring, causing a fire at the marine terminal (Source: @eRadarrua/93884).
Belarusian officials claimed 116 Ukrainian drone incursions in one week, a claim dismissed by Ukrainian authorities (Source: @UkraineNow/81476).
Ukrainian Ministry of Internal Affairs reported 70‑100 civilian deaths per week from Russian drone, ballistic and cruise missile attacks (Source: @UkraineNow/81472).
Russian forces captured the villages of Vozdvizhevka (Zaporizhzhia) and Granov (Kharkiv) (Source: @tass_agency/377761).
sections.threat_assessment: The intensity of UAV operations has escalated, with both sides fielding large numbers of drones and counter‑UAV assets. Russian use of Belarusian territory for drone launches threatens critical western supply lines, while Ukraine's robust air defense has mitigated many attacks but sustains material losses. Repeated strikes on logistics hubs such as the UN depot and the Tuapse refinery indicate a strategic focus on disrupting supply chains and energy infrastructure.
sections.regional_response: Ukraine continues to mobilize air defense and ground forces across all fronts, reporting advances on the Sumy and Kharkiv fronts while sustaining heavy civilian casualties. Russia consolidates territorial gains in the Zaporizhzhia and Kharkiv regions and intensifies strikes on logistical targets. Belarusian claims of Ukrainian drone incursions appear to be part of information operations, with no corroborated evidence of significant Ukrainian activity from Belarus.
sections.intelligence_notes: Monitoring should prioritize UAV flight paths from Belarus toward the Kiev‑Chop corridor and assess the effectiveness of Ukrainian air defenses against high‑volume drone attacks. Continued surveillance of Russian strike patterns on logistics nodes, especially in the south and east, is essential for anticipating supply disruptions. Civilian casualty trends underscore the need for enhanced air‑raid warning systems and civilian shelter provisions in high‑risk zones.
